content:
Most Angular Features Are More Exposed Than Teams Realize

7 min read
4 hours ago

--

Press enter or click to view image in full size

A typical Angular feature might begin like this:
features/
└── customers/
├── components/
├── pages/
├── services/
├── store/
├── models/
├── customer.routes.ts
└── customer.facade.tsThe structure looks clean.
Everything related to customers lives together.
The feature appears isolated.
Then another feature needs customer information.
A developer writes:
import { CustomerStore } from
'../customers/store/customer.store';Another developer needs a model:
import { Customer } from
'../customers/models/customer.model';Then someone imports a component directly:
import { CustomerBadgeComponent } from
'../customers/components/customer-badge.component';Every import solves an immediate problem.
But together, they gradually remove the feature boundary.
The folder still exists.
